label.name: Copyright Control
label.profile:
[b]Please use the "Published By" field when "Copyright Control" is credited that way, also when it is mentioned without a specific credit. When a release states "© Copyright Control" please use the "Copyright (c)" field[/b].
"Copyright Control" means there is no publisher known for the composition(s) featured on a release. It is used in lieu of there being a known publisher.
Also referred to as: - C.C. - C. C. - CC - C/C - Co. Co. - Contr. Copyr. - Copyr. contr. - Contr. Copy - Contr. Cop. - Cop. Con. - COP.CON. - COP.CONTR. - Cop. Cont. - C. Control - Cop. Control - Copy Right Control - Copyright control - Copyright Controle - Copyright Control Worldwide - Control - Copyright Controlled - Copyrigth Control - CopyControl - Copy Control - CRC (not to be confused with the CRC often printed on [l=Columbia House] record club editions.)

label.name: Essex
label.profile:
Publisher. Use for releases which credit "Essex" only.
For the [a=Bill Haley And His Comets] related credits, see [l=Essex Records (3)].

label.name: Marcy Music
label.contactinfo: 6 Rue du Président Georges Pompidou 93440 DUGNY, France
label.profile: French Publisher. Manager : Alain Donnat

label.name: Novalis
label.profile: Swiss label Label Code: LC 1122 / LC 01122

label.name: Membran
label.contactinfo:
Membran Media GmbH a Membran Entertainment Group Company Langenhorner Chaussee 44a 22335 Hamburg Federal Republic of Germany
Tel: +49 (0) 40-4711 0100 E-Mail: info(at)membran.net
label.profile: German label established 1969 in Hamburg. [b]Label Code: LC 29284 [/b] .

label.name: Devonshire Studios
label.contactinfo:
Devonshire Sound Studios 10733 Magnolia Blvd. North Hollywood, CA 91601
(Contact info now obsolete)
label.profile:
Devonshire Studios, North Hollywood, CA, USA
Originally located in Granada Hills, California. The expanded studio in nearby North Hollywood was built and owned by [a=Dave Mancini].
Also referred to as: - Devonshire Audio/Video Studio - Devonshire Audio & Video Studios, Burbank - Devonshire Audio & Video Studios, LA - Devonshire Audio Visual - Devonshire Productions - Devonshire Recording Studio - Devonshire Recording Studios, North Hollywood, CA - Devonshire Sound - Devonshire Sound Studio - Devonshire Sound Studios - Devonshire Studio
Engineer known to have worked here: - [a=Gary Boatner] (1986-1991)

label.name: Birdland
label.profile:
Also known as "The Jazz Corner of the World". Legendary 1949 to 1965 New York jazz club.
For the current jazz club in New York City called Birdland, please use: [l1261938].
[b]Do not confuse with the [l=Birdland Series] by [l=Roulette Records][/b]
Birdland opened on December 15, 1949 at 1678 Broadway, just north of West 52nd Street. Named Birdland after Charlie Parker's nickname to cash in on his popularity. Parker himself only performed in the club on a few occasions. The club mainly billed bop artist and many of these released live recordings from Birdland. In 1959 one of the original nine owners, Irving Levy, was stabbed to death at the club. In 1964 the club filed for bankruptcy. One of the creditors was [a=Gerry Mulligan]. An attempt at booking more traditional jazz artists didn't profit and the original club closed in 1965. Some 20 years later - in 1986 - a new club named Birdland opened in 2745 Broadway at 105th Street, in 1997 re-located to 315 West 44th Street, between 8th & 9th Avenues. Also in 1986 jazz drummer [a=Al Williams (8)] opened [l=Birdland West] in Long Beach, California.

label.name: Suzelle
label.profile: French label and publisher associated with [a422189]. Also see [l413722].

label.name: Harry Smith Recording Studios
label.contactinfo:
2 West 46th Street New York, N.Y. (contact info now obsolete)
156 West 44th Street New York, N.Y. ME3-2996 (contact info now obsolete)
label.profile:
Defunct recording studio in New York of recording engineer [a=Harry Smith (4)], founded in the 1930s. Was the first independent recording studio on the U.S. East Coast. Taken over in 1944 by Fred Hall and Chuck Phillips.
Notable as the location of Frank Sinatra's first-ever recording, performing with Frank Mane's band, on March 18, 1939.
May appear on releases as: Harry Smith Studios, NYC Harry Smith Studios, New York City A Harry Smith Recording Harry Smith Recording Studio, NYC The Harry Smith Studio

label.name: Manus
label.profile:
"Manus" means there is no publisher known for the composition(s) featured on a release. It is not a publisher, but information about the lack of a (known) publisher. Manus should be entered as 'published by' on releases, even if it actually means it is published without a (known) publishing company.
Swedish, Norwegian and Finnish releases.
See also [l48207] for other / international releases.

label.name: Claves Records
label.contactinfo:
Claves Records SA Chemin des Passiaux 15-17 1008 Prilly Switzerland
Tel: +41 21 721 13 36 Fax: +41 21 721 13 37 E-Mail: info(at)claves.ch
label.profile: Swiss record company; please also see: [l1659658]. For the brand/label, please use [l=Claves].

label.name: April Music
label.profile:
International music publishing company affiliated with ASCAP. Also seen on releases as: - April Msc. - April Mus.
[b]Please, consider also [l1503215], [l264623], [l269113], [l273076], [l883729], [l681956], [l2052154] if stated that way on the release[/b].

label.name: Kingsway Hall
label.contactinfo: 75 Kingsway Street, London
label.profile: The Kingsway Hall, Holborn, London, built in 1912, was the home of the West London Mission of the Methodist Church, and eventually became one of the most important recording venues for classical music and film music in the UK. Used by [l=EMI] from 1933; their earliest stereo sessions for ([a=Sergei Prokofiev]'s "Symphony No.7", conducted by [a=Nicolai Malko]) were recorded here on 7 February 1955. Their main UK rival, [l5320], also used the facility. It was demolished in 1998 to make way for a hotel of the same name, which opened in 2000. The hotel’s reception desk is on the approximate location where orchestra members once recorded.

label.name: Manor Mobile
label.profile:
Mobile recording studio owned by [a=Richard Branson] in the mid 1970s until the late 1980s. It was based at [l=The Manor] studio, Shipton-on-Cherwell, north of Oxford, UK, during that time.
Also appears as: The Manor Mobile The Manor Mobile Unit The Manor Mobile Recording Unit Monor Mobile de Londres

label.name: Chrysalis Music Ltd.
label.contactinfo:
Registered office address since 17 December 2024: Floors 1-3 20 Vauxhall Bridge Road LONDON SW1V 2SA United Kingdom
(all obsolete): 8th Floor 5 Merchant Square London W2 1AS United Kingdom
33 Wigmore Street London, W1U 1QX
The Chrysalis Building, Bramley Road London, W10 6SP
London House, 53/54 Haymarket, London, SW1Y 4RP
9 Jermyn Street, London, SW1Y 6JE
label.profile:
British publishing company, not a label. Incorporated 18 September 1968. Affiliated with PRS (IPI #39991729).
Full legal entity name: Chrysalis Music Limited UK Company number: 00938985 Company status: Dormant
After buying out [a=Terry Ellis] in 1985, [a=Chris Wright] sold [l=Chrysalis] Records to [l=EMI] in 1991. He retained the music publishing business Chrysalis Music Ltd until 2010 when it was sold to [l=BMG Rights Management (UK) Limited].
Often releases will credit "[l113559]" for publishing. In these cases, please credit as on the release. Please also consider [l811858]
May also appear as: - Chrysalis Music Ltd - Chrysalis Music Limited - Chrysalis M. Ltd.

label.name: Edel Classics GmbH
label.profile:
German record company, specializing in classical music. Legally operated under this name from 18 January 2001 to 31 May 2010.
Managed by Dr. André Fred Finkenwirth until 24 April 2002, when [a2303257] and Jens Quindt became the new Directors.
Full legal entity name: edel classics GmbH Hamburg company number: HRB 65138 Registered: 19 August 1997 as "Ideal" Verlag GmbH Parent company: [l1631770] from 8 September 1997 Name change: 18 January 2001 - this page Dissolved: 31 May 2010 (merged into [l168010])

label.name: Francis Day
label.profile:
Generic publishing credit associated with [l=Francis, Day & Hunter Ltd.] ([l264377]) or affiliated companies. The catalogue has been owned by EMI Music Publishing Ltd. since 1974.
Please also consider: - [l=Francis Day Music] - [l=Publications Francis Day] ([l279224]) - [l=Editions Francis Day] - ed. Francis-Day - Éditions Francis Day - Francis - [l537005] - Fr. Day
Please use [l=Francis Day S.A.] for the South African entity.

label.name: Forde Abbey
label.contactinfo:
Forde Abbey House and Gardens Chard, Somerset TA20 4LU United Kingdom
Telephone 01460 220231 e-mail: info @ fordeabbey.co.uk
label.profile: A privately owned former Cistercian monastery in Somerset, England, used as a venue for classical performances. In some credits, it will be called Forde Abbey, Chard, Dorset. That is their postal address location and not the abbey's physical location.

label.name: Maria Minor, Utrecht
label.contactinfo: Schuilkerk Maria Minor Achter Clarenburg 6a 3511 JJ Utrecht Nederland / Netherlands
label.profile: An old parish church in Utrecht, The Netherlands. Located at Achter Clarenburg 6a, right next to Hoog Catharijne shopping mall. Nowadays it’s in use as Café Olivier. Also known as Oud Katholieke Kerk. Spanish name: Iglesia de Santa María Minor

label.name: Studio Guillaume Tell
label.contactinfo: Studio Guillaume Tell, 20 Avenue de la Belle Gabrielle, 92150 Suresnes, Paris, France Phone : +33 (0)1 42 04 05 05 Fax : +33 (0)1 42 04 68 69 Contact : Roland Guillotel Email: booking@guillaumetell.com Tech Support : Denis Caribaux guillaumetell@wanadoo.fr
label.profile:
French studio founded by [a=Roland Guillotel] located in the Paris suburb of Suresnes. Many French and international artist have recorded here, including [a=Depeche Mode], [a=Alex Firla], [a=Stuart Bruce], [a=Didier Weiss], [a=Alain Lubrano], [a=Rodolphe Sanguinetti] and [a=Véronique Sanson].
Also referred to as: - Guillaume Tell Studio - Guillaume Tell Studios - Studios Guillaume Tell - Studio Guillame Tell, Paris, France - Studio Guillhaume Tell, Paris

label.name: Henry Wood Hall, London
label.contactinfo:
Trinity Church Square, London, England SE1 4HU UK
44 20 7403 0118
label.profile:
Redundant church and orchestral rehearsal and recording venue located in Trinity Church Square, Southwark, London, England, UK, named after the conductor Sir Henry Wood. Formerly the Holy Trinity Church, it was designed in 1823–24 by Francis Octavius Bedford.
Also appears as: - Sir Henry Wood Hall, London

label.name: Chartmakers
label.contactinfo: Paimenenkallio 3b 02300 Espoo, Finland Tel. +358 400 474824 e-mail: svante@chartmakers.fi
label.profile:
Finnish mastering studio, established in 2004. Run by mastering engineer [a=Svante Forsbäck].
Name variations as found on this profile: - Chartmakers - Chartmakers Oy - Chartmakers, Helsinki - Chartmakers Audio - Chartmakers Mastering

label.name: St Martin's Church, East Woodhay, Hampshire
label.contactinfo: St Martin‘s Church East Woodhay Hampshire RG20 0AL

label.name: Takomo Studiot
label.contactinfo: (Contact info now obsolete) Takomotie 3 00380 Helsinki 38 Finland
label.profile: Finnish sound studio established by [l=Musiikki-Fazer], built in 1977. Studio was designed and constructed by [l=Westlake Audio] (Tom Hidley). The studio was closed in the late 1980s, and the building has been demolished since.

label.name: Studio Lukaskirche, Dresden
label.contactinfo:
Lukaskirche Lukasplatz 1 01069 Dresden Deutschland / Germany
Tel: #49 (0) 351-476 980 E-mail: kg.dresden_lukas[at]evlks.de
label.profile:
Evangelical-Lutheran church in Dresden, Germany, constructed from 1899 to 1903. Heavily damaged during the bombing of Dresden before being reconstructed from 1964 to 1972. In addition to serving as a church, the building is also used as a recording studio and concert venue, especially for classical music.
Name variations: [i]- Lukas-Kirche, Dresden - Lukaskirche, Dresden[/I]

label.name: Doopsgezinde Kerk, Haarlem
label.contactinfo:
Doopsgezinde Kerk Frankestraat 24 2011 HV Haarlem Holland
Tel: #31 (0) 23 5321883 E-Mail: administratie(at)vdgh.nl
label.profile: Mennonite church in classical Dutch style from 1683, situated in the City of Haarlem, Holland. Name variations: Doopsgezinde Kerk Haarlem Doopsgezinde Kerk in Haarlem, The Netherlands Vereenigde Doopsgezinde Kerk, Haarlem, Netherlands

label.name: Studio Christuskirche, Berlin
label.contactinfo: Christuskirche Berlin-Oberschöneweide Firlstraße 16 12459 Berlin Germany
label.profile: [i]Name variations: Christuskirche Oberschöneweide, Berlin; Christuskirche, Berlin-Köpenick[/i]. [b]Not to be confused with the [l280422], which also serves as a recording studio, mainly for [l7703]![/b] Church from 1908 in the Oberschöneweide district in Berlin, Germany. From 1959 to 2001, the church was used as a recording studio because of its acoustic advantages. During this time, many classical recordings were made in the building, mainly by [l=VEB Deutsche Schallplatten Berlin] for the label [l47734]. Also known as: [l1839815]

label.name: Studio Paul-Gerhardt-Kirche, Leipzig
label.contactinfo:
Paul-Gerhardt-Kirche Selneckerstr. 7 04277 Leipzig Federal Republic of Germany
Tel: #49 (0) 341 301 2000 Fax: #49 (0) 341 391 8664 E-Mail: kg.leipzig_connewitz_loessnig@evlks.de
label.profile: Evangelical Lutheran church situated in the district Connewitz of the City of Leipzig, Eastern Germany. The church was used as recording studio for classical recordings by East German labels [l6621] and [l47734].

label.name: Bonnier Music Publishing
label.profile:
Publishing arm of [l=Bonnier Music]
Name variations as found on this profile: - Bonnier Music Publishing - Bonnier Music Publishing AB - Bonnier Publishing

label.name: Universal Motown Records
label.profile:
US record company.
For the label, please use [l71518].

label.name: Studio 92
label.contactinfo: 3-5 rue Solférino 92100 Boulogne France Tel. : 609.03.11 (Contact info now obsolete)
label.profile: Recording and mastering studios located in Boulogne, near Paris, France (EU). Also appearing as "Studios 92". Engineers : [a=Jean-Claude Charvier] and [a=Jean-Pierre Mareuil] (second mid of the 70’s).

label.name: Audio-Video Studios
label.profile: New York, N.Y.

label.name: The Audio Archiving Company
label.contactinfo: http://www.audioarchiving.co.uk/
label.profile: The Audio Archiving Company was established in October 1997 as a result of the closure of “The Decca Recording Centre”, by employees who between them had over 40 years experience within the recording industry. Engineers working at the company are [a376984], [a1242028] and [a401527] -- Also called The Audio Archiving Company Limited

label.name: Herkulessaal, München
label.contactinfo:
Verwaltung der Residenz München Residenzstraße 1 80333 München Germany
Tel: #49 (0) 89 2 90 67-1 Fax: #49 (0) 89 2 90 67-2 25 E-Mail: residenzmuenchen[at]bsv.bayern.de
label.profile: Concert hall in the Munich palace ([l510306]), former royal palace of the Bavarian monarchs in the city center of Munich ([i]München[/i]), Germany. Also know as [i]Herkules Residential Hall[/i] or [i]Herkulessaal der Münchner Residenz[/i]; sometimes written [i]Herkules-Saal[/i].

label.name: Van Gelder Studio, Hackensack, New Jersey
label.contactinfo: (obsolete) Van Gelder Studio 25 Prospect Ave. Hackensack, NJ 07601 U.S.A.
label.profile: Recording Studio operated by engineer [a=Rudy Van Gelder] at this location from 1947 to mid-July 1959, when he moved his operations to Englewood Cliffs: [l=Van Gelder Studio, Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey] and ceased his day job as an optometrist. The original studio doubled as his parent's living room, but the house (built 1945-46) had been designed with the intention RVG would be able to use it as a recording space. The room's ceiling was higher than usual to improve acoustics and a control room was built behind a double-glazed window. Initially the facility was used to record family and friends, but Van Gelder's activities soon spread to recording local musicians. Eventually, in 1952 tenor saxophonist [a227155] played a recording of his session engineered by Van Gelder to [a252962] of [l281] who soon switched his label's recording needs to the Hackensack studio. RVG was among the first American engineers to use a Neumann condenser microphone and the Ampex 300 tape recorder, both introduced in 1949. From 1953, RVG was able to undertake his own mastering for commercial clients. NOTE: Many liner notes on 1950s albums engineered by Van Gelder falsely indicate "New York" as the recording location.

label.name: Air Chrysalis Scandinavia AB
label.profile:
Air Chrysalis Scandinavia AB was a publishing administration company, affiliated with STIM (IPI #260271691). There's also a publisher known as [l265339].
The works now form part of the catalogue of [l1536174].
Name variations as found on this profile: - Air Chrysalis Music Scandinavia AB - Air Chrysalis Scandinavia AB - Air-Chrysalis Scandinavia AB - Air Chrysalis Scand. AB

label.name: St. Augustine's Church, Kilburn
label.contactinfo: St. Augustine's Church Kilburn Park Rd North Maida Vale, London NW6 5XB UK
label.profile:
Anglo-Catholic parish church in Neo-Gothic style, situated in the area of Kilburn, North London, United Kingdom. Because of its large scale and ornate architecture, it is sometimes affectionately referred to as "the Cathedral of North London", although the church is not a cathedral in any official sense.
St. Augustine's was founded by Richard Carr Kirkpatrick in the Anglo-Catholic tradition in 1870. By 1871, a foundation stone had been laid and the original 'iron church' was subsequently replaced by a much more ambitious form of religious architecture, a Victorian Gothic church designed by John Loughborough Pearson. It is listed as a Grade I building by English Heritage.

label.name: St. Barnabas Church, London
label.contactinfo: St Barnabas Church Holden Road London N12 7DN
label.profile: St. Barnabas Church, North Finchley, London, England. Sometimes specified as Woodside Park rather than North Finchley. Not to be confused with [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/855690-St-Barnabas-Church-Pimlico-London]St. Barnabas Church, Pimlico, London[/url].

label.name: Vox Productions, Inc.
label.contactinfo:
Obsolete: Vox Productions, Inc. 236 West 55th Street New York 19, N.Y. U.S.A.
Other: Vox Productions, Inc. 211 E. 43rd St., N.Y., N.Y. 10017
label.profile:
US record company The company and the subsidiary label [l=VOX (6)] were launched by [a2716119] in New York City in 1945 to market recordings of European performers and conductors in the US. Vox's licensing deal with the French Polydor label introduced French singers like Edith Piaf and Jacqueline François to a North American audience. The company was also one of the first to produce not only 78 rpm records, but, starting in 1949, also 33 1/3 rpm LPs.
Mr. Mendelssohn-Bartholdy, a fourth-generation descendant of the composer Felix Mendelssohn, sold the company in May 1978 to Ira Moss, owner of [l=The Moss Music Group, Inc.].

label.name: Peermusic AB
label.contactinfo:
Peermusic Aktiebolag Karlsviksgatan 18 112 41 Stockholm Sweden
Phone: +46 (0)8-704 01 40 Email: stockholm@peermusic.com
label.profile:
Peermusic AB was founded in Stockholm on December 28, 1945 and is now a Swedish subsidiary of the holding company [l350429], therefore also a part of the major publisher [l280050]'s group of companies. Affiliated with STIM (IPI #228574451).
Sometimes listed on releases as: Peer Music AB Peermusic AB, Sweden Peermusic Sweden AB

label.name: Jesus-Christus-Kirche, Berlin
label.contactinfo:
Jesus-Christus-Kirche Hittorfstr. 23 14195 Berlin Deutschland / Germany
Tel.: +49 (0) 176 / 34 233 007
label.profile: [i]Name variations: "Jesus-Christus-Kirche", "Jesus Christuskirche Berlin-Dahlem" or "Jesus-Christus-Kirche, Dahlem"[/i] [b]Not to be confused with [l268983], located in the district Oberschöneweide of Berlin.[/b] Protestant church built in 1931, located in the Dahlem district of Berlin, Germany. Because of its acoustic advantages, the church has also been used as a recording studio from the early 1950s. Since then, many classical recordings have been made in the building, mainly for [l7703], but also for many other major labels. Organ: the church was originally equipped with a newly built Sauer organ, which was damaged during the war and replaced in 1971 by a new organ (III/P/45, including swellboxes and a roll-swell) built by Emil Hammer (Hannover), using stops from the previous organ; Hammer also overhauled the instrument in 1989; renovation is planned for 2025/26.

label.name: Scandinavian Songs AB
label.profile:
Publishing company.
Related to: - [l=Scandinavian Songs]

label.name: Universal / Reuter & Reuter Förlag
label.profile:
Publisher
Also appears as: Universal/Reuter-Reuter

label.name: Universal / MGB Scandinavia AB
label.profile:
Name variations as found on this profile: - Universal / MGB Scandinavia AB - Universal/MGB Scandinavia AB
Related to: - [l=Universal Music Publishing MGB Scandinavia AB]

label.name: Shapiro Bernstein Scandinavia
label.profile:
Publisher.
See also [l526519] when credited that way.

label.name: WOR Studios
label.contactinfo: From 1926 to 2005: 1440 Broadway New York, NY
label.profile:
Recording studio related to the NYC radio station of the same name. Associated record labels are [l=WOR Feature] and [l=WOR Recording].
May alternately be credited as: - WOR Recording Studio

label.name: Beltone Studios
label.profile: New York recording and mastering studio. Also shown as: Beltone Recording Studios, Beltone Recording

label.name: Columbia 30th Street Studio
label.profile:
CBS 30th Street Studio, nicknamed "The Church". The 30th Street facility contained both Columbia's "Studio C" and "Studio D".
American recording studio operated by [l93330] from 1949 to 1981 located in Manhattan, New York City, US. Also mentioned just as 30th Street Studio / 30th Street Studios and Columbia Recording Studios, 30th Street, New York.

label.name: All Saints Church Petersham
label.contactinfo: All Saints' Church Bute Avenue Petersham, Richmond TW10 7AX (London Borough of Richmond upon Thames) United Kingdom
label.profile: All Saints' Church, Petersham, London is a former church now used as a private residence. [a288813] had built a studio inside it and used it for the recordings of orchestral film scores, until the building was sold by the Church Commissioners. Not to be confused with the Australian All Saints Anglican Church, Petersham.

label.name: Editions Marouani
label.contactinfo: 176, avenue Charles-de-Gaulle 92200 Neuilly-sur-Seine 01 46 37 86 66 charleymarouani@wanadoo.fr
label.profile:
Publishing company, see also [l2451773], [l=Editions Gilbert Marouani], [l=Marouani], [l1648339].
Founded in 1971 by Gilbert Marouani in 37 rue Marbeuf 75008 Paris On February 2005, relocated 176, avenue Charles-de-Gaulle, 92200 Neuilly-sur-Seine

label.name: RCA Studio A
label.profile: Located in New York City, U.S.A. A.k.a. RCA Victor's Studio A, New York City or RCA Studios, New York

label.name: Allo Music
label.contactinfo: 10, rue de la Grande Batelière 75009 PARIS, France Phone : (+33) 01 53 24 64 64
label.profile: French music publisher. May also appear as - Edition Allo Music - Editions Allo Music - Ed. Allo Music or any other variation

label.name: SLG, LLC
label.contactinfo:
[2016] SLG, LLC P.O. Box 472 15332 Antioch Street Pacific Palisades, CA 90272 U.S.A.
[2011] SLG, LLC 1316 3rd Street Promenade, Suite 111 Santa Monica, CA 90401
[2004] SLG, LLC 429 Santa Monica Boulevard, Suite 330 Santa Monica, CA 90405
label.profile: Registered company name of [l=Savoy Label Group], please use that profile if it's printed as such.

label.name: Santa Monica Sound Recorders
label.profile: Recording and mixing studio(s) in Santa Monica, California. Also appears as "Santa Monica Sound".

label.name: Studio De La Grande Armée
label.contactinfo: [l=Palais des Congrès] CIP 96 75 853 PARIS CEDEX 17 France
label.profile:
Studio established by Jean-Claude Dubois in 1972.
Name variants for this profile: - Studio De La Grande Armée - studios de la grande arme'e - Studio Grand Armée,Paris

label.name: Usher Hall, Edinburgh
label.contactinfo: Lothian Road Edinburgh, Midlothian EH1 2EA UK
label.profile: Venue in Edinburgh, Scotland.

label.name: Elkan & Schildknecht
label.profile:
A Swedish publisher with a changed name to Universal/Elkan & Schildknecht, and later to Elkan & Schildknecht Carelius. And then, with that new name [b]Elkan & Schildknecht Carelius[/b], representing one of Universal Music Publishing's many catalogues. A special note: Anders Carelius was the owner of Elkan & Schildknecht at the time when the publishing company was sold to Universal in 2008. [a=Evert Taube] is an example of an artist with many songs in this publisher's catalogue.
Company status for Universal/Elkan & Schildknecht AB: Not active as a company after a fusion within Universal. Universal/Elkan & Schildknecht AB was active between May 22, 2008 and January 23, 2014.
Also see sister profile [l=Elkan & Schildknecht AB]
Name variation: - Elkan & Schildknecht Music - Elkan-Schildknecht

label.name: Wiener Bohème Verlag GmbH
label.profile:
German publishing company with full legal name, affiliated with GEMA (IPI #32884379). Operating from 30 September 1958 to 25 October 2007 (merged).
For the common entry, please visit [l387735].
Full legal entity name: Wiener Bohème Verlag Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung Registered (Berlin company HRB 806): 30 September 1958 (roots of the company go back to 1931) Re-registered (Munich company HRB 98905): 13 May 1992 Merged in: 2 September 2005 [l2603728] and [l1917536] Dissolved (merged): 25 October 2007 with [l852874]

label.name: Universal / MCA Music Publishing Scandinavia AB
label.contactinfo:
Universal Music Publishing AB Banérgatan 16 11483 Stockholm Sweden
Phone: +46 (0)8 629 53 00
Email: ump.sweden@umusic.com
label.profile:
Swedish publisher.
Name appearances as found on this profile: - Universal / MCA Music Publishing Scandinavia AB - Universal / MCA Music Scandinavia - Universal / MCA Music Scand. AB - Universal/MCA Music Scandinavia - Universal/MCA Music Scandinavia AB - Universal/MCA Music Scandinavia AB, Stockholm - Universal/MCA Music Scand. - Universal/MCA Music Scand AB - Universal/MCA Music Publishing AB - Universal MCA Music Scandinavia AB

label.name: United Sound Systems
label.contactinfo: 5840 Second Avenue Detroit, MI 48202 Phone: (313) 833-1833 Fax: (313) 833-1250
label.profile:
Recording and mastering studio founded in 1937 by violinist and sound engineer James V. Siracuse (1903-1988) in Detroit, Michigan, USA. [b]For the associated label, see [l1300210].[/b]
It moved to its current location at Second Avenue somewhere during the 1940s. United Sound Systems first independent and full service major recording studio in the United States, and hosted sessions for such acts as [a48432], [a29923], [a267854], and [a115131], among others. [a=Berry Gordy] recorded his first songs here and before the installation of a mastering lab at [l=Hitsville USA Studios, Detroit], this is the place where many [l=Motown] records were cut. In 1972 [l24936] producer [a248249] purchased the studio. The studio slid into decay in the late 20th century and eventually closed in 2006. After being abandoned for a long while, it was purchased by postal worker Danielle Scott and after several years of restoration reopened in 2014. After being threatened by a planned highway expansion, it was designated a historical district in 2015 after efforts from the Detroit Sound Conservancy nonprofit, which took its home in the building. It operated as both a museum and recording studio until it was put up for sale again in 2018. A year later it ws purchased by the Michigan Department of Transportation with the intention of moving and preserving it.
Also appears on releases as: - United Sound - United Sound Studios - United Sounds Studios - United Sound Systems Recording Studios - United Sound Stystems, Inc. - United USS
Known engineers: [a=Ed Wolfrum] (chief), [a=Mike Iacopelli], [a=Dave Palmer (2)], [a=Jim Vitti], [a=Ron Murphy], [a=Greg Ward], [a=Ken Sands], [a=Jerry Estes], Tom Garbe. Studio manager: Chynita Richards (after 2014)

label.name: Universal/Polygram Music Publishing
label.profile: Publishing credits. Variations: - Universal / Polygram Music Publishing - Universal-Polygram Music Publishing

label.name: Bug Music Scandinavia
label.profile: Swedish publisher.

label.name: EMI Catalogue Partner Scandinavia AB
label.profile:
Name variations as found on this profile: - EMI Catalogue Partner Scandinavia AB - EMI Catalogue Partners Scandinavia AB - EMI Catalogue Partner Scand.AB - EMI Catalogue Partner Scandinavia
Related to: - [l=EMI Catalogue Partnership Scandinavia AB]

label.name: Teldec-Studio, Berlin
label.contactinfo: Finckensteinallee 36 1000 Berlin 45 (later: 12205 Berlin) Germany Tel.: (30) 83 00 05-0 Telefax: (30) 83 00 05-49 [obsolete]
label.profile:
Recording and mastering studios of record companies Telefunken and TELDEC, established in the early 1950s in Berlin Lichterfelde. Lacquer cutting lasted until 1981. Credit the studio with Lacquer Cut At (when in combination with an engineer code e.g. A-B-I). By the end of 1981, four Neumann DMM lathes were installed, replacing the old machines. Credit the studio with Mastered At (when in combination with an engineer code e.g. Pü-B-II).
Studio services: recording, mixing, dubbing, editing, disk and tape mastering, library of sound effects, echo chambers, custom cassette duplication. The control room was equipped with a 16-track Teldec/Telefunken console (later enhanced to 24 tracks).
The facilties also included technical research and development laboratories, headed by Horst Redlich. There, a.o., the variable pitch control technology was finalized, Teldec's stereo LP records were developed (1955), an 8-channel tape recorder was constructed (1959), and the high density recording process was developed (1970), paving the way for the Mini-Disc and for DMM. And, together with the Georg Neumann GmbH, the laboratories also developed and advanced new cutting equipment, leading to Neumann's lathes VMS 66, VMS 70, VMS 80 and the DMM lathe VMS 82.
After the decision to close the studios, former studio employees and recording engineers Martin Sauer, Tobias Lehmann and Friedemann Engelbrecht rented and operated the studio complex until 2002, and then renamed it [l=Teldex Studio, Berlin].
For the studio in Hamburg use [l=TELDEC-Studio, Hamburg]
Mastering / cutting engineers: [a=Horst Angenendt] > A-B-I, A-B-II, A-B-III, A-B-IV [a=Sönke Bahns] > B-B-I, B-B-II [a=Christian Stegmaier] > CS-B-I, CS-B-II, CS-B-III [a=Heinz Lauterbach] > Lt, Lt-B, Lt-B-I, Lt-B-II, Lt-B-III [a=Carsten Püschel] > Pü-B-I, Pü-B-II, Pü-B-III Occasionally: [a=Karl Schick] > S-I-B, S-II-B
Note: It was standing procedure for the studio that the cutting engineers added their scribes to both sides (see example [url=https://www.discogs.com/release/208410-Front-242-No-Comment]here[/url]). If a scribe is missing on one vinyl side (see example [url=https://www.discogs.com/release/9873753-Talking-Heads-Speaking-In-Tongues]here[/url]), the release should be separate.
Recording engineers: [a=Martin Fouqué], also chief engineer in the 1970s [a=Eberhard Sengpiel]
Audio engineers (research and development): Horst G. Redlich Hans-Joachim Klemp Gerhard Dickopp Eduard Schüller Walter Bruch
Runout scribes of so far unconfirmed mastering / cutting engineers that may have been working in the Berlin studios from the 1950s and 1960s: G.- (example: [r=25590628]) - potentially Herbert Grenzebach Li (example: [r=25578454]) - check if not Lt. for Heinz Lauterbach L. or Lu. (example: [r=12645751]) - potentially Adalbert Lutter O. (example: [r=12399002]) - potentially Hansgeorg Otto

label.name: Walthamstow Assembly Hall
label.contactinfo: Town Hall, Forest Rd, London, England E17 4JF UK
label.profile: Concert and recording venue located in Walthamstow, London Borough of Waltham Forest, London, England, UK. Built in 1942. Also called Walthamstow Town Hall.

label.name: Alte Kirche, Fautenbach
label.profile: A german church in 77855 Achern-Fautenbach.

label.name: Magic Studio
label.contactinfo: Magic Studio 36 rue Ferdinand Malet 07130 Saint Péray France
label.profile: French recording studio since 2004 based in Saint Péray, Ardèche, founded by sound engineer [a4408419]. For the Belgian studio, associated with [a=Peter Neefs] credits, please use [l304091].

label.name: Palais Des Sports, Paris
label.profile: An indoor sports arena, located at 1, place de la Porte de Versailles in the 15th arrondissement of Paris, France.

label.name: Muziekcentrum Frits Philips
label.contactinfo: Jan van Lieshoutstraat 3 5600 AX Eindhoven The Netherlands
label.profile: Dutch concert hall in Eindhoven opened in 1992.

label.name: Hervormde Kerk Rhoon
label.profile: Dutch church, where many concerts are given. It has a famous organ.

label.name: Doopsgezinde Remonstrantse Kerk Deventer
label.profile: Dutch church in Deventer. This building/church is used for services for the Doopgezinden (Mennonites) as well as for the Remonstranten (Remonstrants). Due to his excellent acoustic, there are many concerts organized.

label.name: Nordiska Musikförlaget
label.contactinfo: Aktiebolaget Nordiska Musikförlaget c/o [l275405] Box 5164 102 44 Stockholm
label.profile:
Swedish music publisher, affiliated with [l218737] (IPI #537208261).
Nordiska Musikförlaget was founded in 1915 by Danish [l3932097]. In 1989, Nordiska Musikförlaget was sold to Finnish [l146229], which was subsequently acquired by Swedish [l275405] in 1997. Nordiska Musikförlaget was thereafter transformed into a limited company on June 10, 2011, under the name [l275702], and with a new articles of association on May 12, 2024.
Name variants and related profiles: - Nordiska Musikkforlaget - [l282286] - [l275702]

label.name: Éditions Tutti
label.contactinfo:
Société Nouvelle Des Éditions Musicales Tutti 15, rue Saussier-Leroy PARIS 17e Tel: 924-83-89
And before: 46, rue Laffitte Paris - 9e
label.profile:
Société Nouvelle Des Éditions Musicales Tutti, a French music publisher.
May also appear as: - Tutti Societe - Tutti, ed. Tutti, Société Tutti, ...
Headed by Régis Talar Became now [l=Warner Chappell Music France] Before known as [l=Éditions Philippe Parès]

label.name: Éditions Bagatelle
label.contactinfo: 10, rue Washington - Paris 8e - France
label.profile: Phonogram Producer/Publisher Also appears as [l156860]. Started by [a500236] in 1962. Editions Bagatelle joined Sacem on June 19th, 1973 and became a permanent member on April 17th, 1980.

label.name: Nieuwe Kerk, The Hague
label.contactinfo: CONTACT CHURCH / CONCERTS De Nieuwe Kerk Spui 175 2511 BM The Hague, The Netherlands T: 0031 70 88 00 300
label.profile: Recording location. Music and dance forms are the main focus in the Nieuwe Kerk. There is a wide range within genres such as opera, pop music, family shows and entertainment. The Nieuwe Kerk in The Hague is the perfect location to organize a business event or a private occasion. The Nieuwe Kerk is within walking distance of, for example, the Binnenhof and directly across the street from the city hall of The Hague. The Nieuwe Kerk is ideal for a number of simple reasons. The Nieuwe Kerk is full of character, very representative, centrally located and therefore easily accessible by car and public transport. Suitable for meetings of 150-700 people.

label.name: Stainer & Bell
label.contactinfo: Stainer & Bell Ltd PO Box 110, Victoria House 23 Gruneisen Road London, England N3 1DZ
label.profile: English music publishing house. Publisher of Purcell Society offerings. From website: "Founded in 1907, Stainer & Bell is an independent, family-run business, specialising in the publication of quality music and books. The catalogue includes choral, orchestral, vocal and instrumental music representing the highest achievement of British classical composers from the sixteenth century to the present, a range of internationally esteemed collected editions, and an unrivalled collection of distinguished contemporary hymnody."

label.name: Scandia Studio
label.contactinfo: Stuurenkatu 4 Helsinki 51, Finland Cable: SCANDIAMUSIC
label.profile:
Finnish recording studio founded in 1961.
Some people who worked in there were [a=Jouko Ahera] and [a=Pentti Pöyry].

label.name: Finnlevy Studios
label.profile:
Recording and production studio in Helsinki, Finland, associated with the label [l39812].
Engineers known to have worked here: [a590466], [a524222].

label.name: Hans Busch Musikförlag AB
label.profile:
Swedish publishing company run by [a1489176]. Also printed as Hans Busch's Mflg, also as Hans Busch's
See also [l2373595] and [l424450].

label.name: Kirche Blumenstein
label.contactinfo: Ev. Ref Kirchgemeinde Blumenstein – Pohlern Kirchenstrasse 7 3638 Blumenstein Schweiz / Switzerland
label.profile: Church in the town of Blumenstein, canton of Bern, district of Thun, Switzerland. It was constructed in 1285 and consecrated to St. Nicholas.

label.name: Spanka Music
label.profile:
The BMI publishing house owned and operated by the father of [a=Paul Anka], Andy Anka. The associated ASCAP publishing house was called "Flanka".
As noted on page 4 of the 2 July 1966 issue of Billboard: "The firms, which were founded in 1958, had been based largely on songs written by Paul Anka, Andy Anka's son. Current catalog has 400 - 500 titles."
Please consider [l=Spanka], [l=Spanka Pub.], [l=Spanka Music Co.], [l=Spanka Music Ltd.], [l=Spanka Music Corp.], [l=Spanka Music, Inc.], if so credited.

label.name: EDC, Germany
label.contactinfo:
[defunct] EDC GmbH Emil-Berliner-Straße 13 D-30851 Langenhagen Germany
Phone: +49 (0) 511 972 0 Fax: +49 (0) 511 972 1355 E-Mail: info.de@edcllc.com
label.profile:
[b]E[/b]ntertainment [b]D[/b]istribution [b]C[/b]ompany GmbH was a German manufacturer of CD, DVD, Blu-ray and vinyl records established in May 2005 following the acquisition of the manufacturing and distribution facilities of the Universal Music Group. The predecessor for this plant was [l=Universal M & L, Germany]. EDC was the largest, fully integrated optical disc manufacturing and distribution site in Europe and commenced pressing vinyl records in late 2015. Seven Toolex Alpha presses were installed, with a capacity of 15 million records per year The company declared bankruptcy in and ceased operations at the end of 2016. Its equipment was auctioned in May 2017.
Please enter all pressings here that show "[b]Made in Germany by EDC[/b]" (on releases since approx. 2009 in capital letters) and additional letters ranging from A to ZZ on the opposite side of the sentence in the inner CD ring. Also be aware that since sometime in the 2010s, the "MADE IN GERMANY BY EDC" text may no longer be present, but all other matrix elements including the A-ZZ letter can still be used to identify this manufacturer.
Please be aware that the EDC factory produced CD and CDr media (often for brokers who are sometimes credited in the mirror band or clamping ring) but used their usual CD matrix scheme and SID code pairings.
When entering EDC, Germany in the LCCN section of a release page, add the sequential number "5xxxxxxx", which comes after the "[°]" element in the matrix, as the LCCN number. Please use "Made By" role when that text is present in matrix/inner ring (example release: [r3870286]).
[u]Approx. sequential glass master numbers with corresponding release years[/u]: 2005: 5163xxxx-5169xxxx ("Made in Germany by EDC" hub stamp since c.August)* 2006: 5168xxxx-5183xxxx 2007: 5182xxxx-5206xxxx (no 51932000-52000xxx) 2008: 5205xxxx-5224xxxx ("Made in Germany by EDC" ended at 5218xxxx) 2009: 5223xxxx-5246xxxx ("MADE IN GERMANY BY EDC" started at 5220xxxx) 2010: 5245xxxx-5266xxxx 2011: 5265xxxx-5285xxxx 2012: 5284xxxx-5318xxxx (no 52994xxx-53099999) 2013: 5317xxxx-5341xxxx (no 53211xxx-53214xxx) 2014: 5340xxxx-5365xxxx (no 53435xxx-53482xxx) 2015: 5364xxxx-5386xxxx 2016: 5385xxxx-5405xxxx 2017: 5404xxxx-5407xxxx (ended c.April) *sequence inherited from Universal M & L, Germany, which ended at approx. 51642xxx <5162xxxx numbers are represses except for some 50968000-51013999 codes which were used for reissues/compilations
[b]CD and CDr Matrix patterns[/b]: 0XXXX XXX XXX-X 0X [°] 5xxxxxxxx (apparently on releases manufactured for Universal Music Group and its labels/companies) 0XXXX XXXXX XXX 0X [°] 5xxxxxxxx (apparently on releases for other labels/companies/clients)
[b]Mastering SID codes and corresponding mid-matrix digit[/b]: [°] = [b]✳[/b] - IFPI LV26 or IFPI LV28 [°] = [b]+[/b] - IFPI LV27 (since late 2007) [°] = [b]/[/b] - IFPI L001 (only a short time in 2005?) [°] = [b]#[/b] - IFPI L008 (2007-2008) and IFPI LZ87 (2012-2016?)* (⚠ can be misread as L287 or L787 !) [°] = [b]P[/b] - IFPI L963 (only a short time 2006-2007 and 2011-2012, very small letters)* [°] = [b]E[/b] - IFPI LQ57 (only a short time 2006-2007 and in 2016)* [°] = [b]C[/b] - IFPI L016 or IFPI L017 (only a short time 2012-2013)*
*glass masters produced by other manufacturers (P=[l=P+O Pallas], E=???, C=[l=Cinram GmbH]) [b]Note:[/b] Please do not credit these manufacturers in LCCN when above SID codes appear in conjunction with EDC, Germany matrix patterns.
[b]Mould SID codes pattern[/b]: IFPI 01XX
[b]DVDs[/b] DVDs manufactured by EDC, Germany can be identified by 6 Universal logos in inner or outer ring and have almost the same matrix pattern as CDs, only with additional number 50 or 51 before 0X. Sometimes two different 5xxxxxxx numbers are present in inner and outer rings, both of which can be entered in LCCN. Please DO NOT enter # 50994213 which appears in inner ring of some DVDs on label side.
[b]Blu-ray[/b] Blu-ray matrixes follow the same pattern as DVDs, including the additional 50 and 51 before 0X (where 50 = layer 0 and 51 = layer 1), with 'E' as the [°] mid-matrix digit. Mastering SID codes found on BDs include: IFPI L021 IFPI LZ08, LZ09 These likely originate from external glass master manufacturers.
[b]LCCN roles without EDC Germany text[/b]: "Pressed By" can be used for all discs with matrix numbers >5164xxxx and mould code IFPI 01** (example [r7925823]). "Glass Mastered At" may only be used for discs with above matrix numbers and mastering codes IFPI LV26, LV27 and L008 (example [r22857041]).
If the matrix patterns DO NOT appear on a release which mentions EDC in matrix, but show a different styled one, check if it's manufactured by either [l=EDC, USA] or [l=EDC Blackburn Ltd]

label.name: Kloster Bronnbach
label.contactinfo:
Kloster Bronnbach Bronnbach 9 97877 Wertheim Germany
Tel. (0 93 42) 9 35 20 20 21 oder 9 35 20 20 24 Fax (0 93 42) 9 35 20 20 29 E-Mail: info[at]kloster-bronnbach.de
label.profile: Cistercian monastery in Southern Germany, founded in 1151.

label.name: Muziekcentrum Enschede
label.contactinfo: Address: Noorderhagen 27, 7511 EK Enschede, The Netherlands Postal address: Postbus 634, 7500 AP Enschede, The Netherlands Mail to: evenementen@wilminktheater.nl
label.profile: Concert hall in Enschede, The Netherlands.

label.name: St. Maria Church, Schwetzingen
label.profile: Catholic church in the German town of Schwetzingen in in the northwest of Baden-Württemberg. Its German name is Marienkirche.

label.name: Dom Zu Brixen
label.profile: Church in the Italian town Brixen (Bressanone) in the Alto Adige (Südtirol) province. Also known as Brixner Dom or Dom Mariae Aufnahme in den Himmel und St. Kassian zu Brixen.

label.name: Wyastone Concert Hall
label.contactinfo:
Wyastone Leys, Monmouth NP25 3SR United Kingdom
Telephone: +44 1600 890 007
Email: sales@wyastone.co.uk
label.profile:
Concert hall of the Nimbus Foundation, [l=Wyastone Leys, Monmouth]. The Nimbus Foundation is operated by Wyastone Estate Limited, which also owns [l=Nimbus Records].
Also credited as: Concert Hall Of The Nimbus Foundation Nimbus Concert Hall, Wyastone Wyastone Leys Nimbus Concert Hall

label.name: Oud Katholieke Kerk, Delft
label.contactinfo: Oud-Katholieke Parochie HH Maria en Ursula Bagijnhof 21 2611 AN Delft The Netherlands
label.profile: Catholic church in Delft, The Netherlands; Dutch church name is Oud-Katholieke Parochie HH Maria en Ursula. May appear in English as Old Catholic Church.

label.name: Zentralsaal, Bamberg
label.contactinfo: Promenadestraße 3 96047 Bamberg Germany
label.profile: Former event and concert hall in Bamberg. In operation until around 1999.

label.name: Orangerie, Darmstadt
label.profile: Castle in the South German town Darmstadt. It was conceived as an orangery.

label.name: Filosofisch Theologisch College van de Societeit van Jezus V.Z.W. Heverlee, Belgium
Huis Overcinge
label.contactinfo: Landgoed Overcinge Linthorst Homanlaan 1 7971 RK Havelte
label.profile: Mansion in Havelte, The Netherlands, with a capacity up to 300 people.

label.name: Palazzo Giusti, Padova
label.profile: Sixteenth century palace in Padova, northern Italy.

label.name: La Monnaie
label.profile: Company operating the [l=Théâtre Royal De La Monnaie, Brussels] along with a symphonic orchestra, a choir (see [a=Orchestre Du Théâtre Royal De La Monnaie] and [a=Orchestre Et Choeurs Du Théâtre Royal De La Monnaie]) and a chamber orchestra.

label.name: Théâtre Royal De La Monnaie, Brussels
label.profile: Opera house in Brussels, Belgium, operated by [l=La Monnaie]/De Munt.

label.name: Muziekcentrum Vredenburg, Utrecht
label.contactinfo:
Address (1979-2007, closed): Muziekcentrum Vredenburg Vredenburgpassage 77 3511 DL Utrecht The Netherlands
Address (interim location 2007-2014): De Rode Doos/Vredenburg Leidsche Rijn J.C. Verthorenpad 100 3541 MV Utrecht The Netherlands
Address (interim location 2007-2014): Leeuwenbergh Gasthuis/Vredenburg Leeuwenbergh Servaasbolwerk 1A 3512 NK Utrecht The Netherlands
label.profile:
Former music venue in Utrecht, The Netherlands with a capacity of 1700 and 300 people.
Opened doors on January 26, 1979. In the period 2007-2014 the venue was thoroughly rebuilt, only the original construction of the main hall was maintained, with the new venue being built around this. Two temporary locations were used in this period. During this time the venue also merged with [l496445] and the new venue [l1097114] opened in July, 2014.
Also sometimes listed on releases as: - The Music Centrum, Utrecht - Music Centre, Utrecht - Musikcentrum Vredenberg, Utrecht

label.name: Palacio de Congresos y Auditios, La Coruna
Auditorio de Galicia, Santiago de Compostela
Franz Liszt Conservatory, Budapest
label.contactinfo: Liszt Ferenc tér 8 1061 Budapest Hungary

label.name: Church Of St George The Martyr, London
label.profile: Parish church in Bloomsbury, London. Not to be confused with the [url=https://www.discogs.com/label/848563-Church-Of-St-George-The-Martyr-Queen-Square-London]Church Of St George The Martyr, Queen Square, London[/url], also known as St George the Martyr, Holborn, London.

label.name: Temple Church, London
label.contactinfo: The Temple Church Temple London EC4Y 7BB United Kingdom
label.profile: The Temple Church, City Of London, located between Fleet Street and the River Thames in London, England. House of [a1112648]. Organ: installed by its builder, Harrison & Harrison Ltd, in 1954.

label.name: Tanday Music
label.profile: French publishing company. Tanday = [a=Sylvie Vartan] & [a=Johnny Hallyday]. Also appears as : - Ed. Tanday, - Les Editions Tanday - Tanday Musique See also label [l344634]

label.name: L.E.M.
label.profile: French publishing company from Paris. It can also be spelled Editions L.E.M. or [l286879].

label.name: Unichappell
label.profile: Publisher, affiliated to BMI. May appear misspelled as -"Unichapel", -"Unichappel", -"UNI-Chappel", -"Unichapell", -"UNI Chappell", -"Uni Chappell", or as -"Uni-Chappell" on releases.

label.name: Beurs van Berlage, Amsterdam
label.contactinfo: Damrak 277 1012 ZJ AMSTERDAM Fax: 0031 20 - 6204701 email: info [@] beursvanberlage.nl

label.name: Brucknerhaus Linz
label.profile: Festival and congress center in Linz, Austria, named after the composer [a=Anton Bruckner], opened on March 23, 1974. Designed by Finnish artchitects Heikki and Kaija Siren. The Brucknerhaus is home of the Brucknersaal, the Stiftersaal and the Keplersaal.

label.name: Conrad
label.profile:
Publishing - affiliated to BMI. Currently controlled by [l=BMG Platinum Songs US]. Imprint held and administered by [l=Hal Leonard Corporation].
Please consider [l=Conrad Music], [l=Conrad Music Co.], [l=Conrad Music Corp.], [l=Conrad Music, Inc.], [l=Conrad Music Pub. Co.], [l=Conrad Music Pub. Co. Inc.], [l=Conrad Publ. Co.], [l=Conrad Publishing], [l=Conrad Publishing Inc.], [l=Conrad Publishing Co. Inc.], if so credited.

label.name: WLP Ltd.
label.contactinfo: Phone: +44 (0) 20 3031 6100 Email: info@whitelabelproductions.co.uk
label.profile: Graphic design agency. See [l849534] for the full name legal entity page.

label.name: Provisual Oy
label.contactinfo: PROVISUAL OY Vilhonvuorenkatu 11 D, 7krs 00500 Helsinki, FINLAND provisual@provisual.fi

label.name: Jare
label.profile: Publishing company, see also [l=Edition Jare].

label.name: Royal Roost (2)
label.profile: The Royal Roost was a jazz club located at 1580 Broadway in New York City. Ralph Watkins originally opened up as a chicken restaurant, but by 1948 the club began to showcase the likes of Charlie Parker, Dizzy Gillespie, and a who's who of the New York jazz scene. In 1949 a records label, [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/35931-Roost]Roost[/url] Records, was founded by Arthur Faden, Bill Faden, Monty Kay and Ralph Watkins which recorded many of the musicians who performed at the club. By the early 1950s the jazz component of the Roost was moved to Watkins's new club, Bop City.

label.name: WCMF Serious Catalogue
label.profile: Also known as Warner/Chappell Music Finland Serious Catalogue. The publishing rights of WCMF Serious Catalogue are now owned by [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/399207-Fennica-Gehrman-Oy]Fennica Gehrman[/url].

label.name: Grizzly Video Music
label.profile: Publisher

label.name: Editions Intersong Paris
label.profile: Publisher

label.name: Editions Claude Pascal
label.profile:
Publishing company created by [a=Claude Pascal] Can also appear as "Claude Pascal Editions SARL", or "Ed. Claude Pascal", or "Ed. Cl. Pascal", or "C. Pascal Ed.", or any other similar variation.
Please consider also : - [l=Les Editions Musicales Claude Pascal] if appearing like that or as "E.M. Claude Pascal" - [l=Claude Pascal] if appearing like that, or as "Cl. Pascal", or "C. Pascal" or...

label.name: Martin Coulter
label.profile: Publisher for [a=Bill Martin & Phil Coulter] on French releases

label.name: Editions Musicales Alain Boublil
label.profile: French Music Publishers; ® 1979 (often shortened to 'Editions Alain Boublil').

label.name: Christ Church, Sutton
label.contactinfo:
Christ Church, Christchurch Park Sutton, London SM2 5TN UK
T: 0208 288 8380 E: christchurch23646@gmail.com
label.profile: Church of England parish church in Sutton, London (formerly Surrey).

label.name: Sylvain Edition AB
label.contactinfo: Edition Sylvain Aktiebolag c/o [l3418768] Box 17169 104 62 Stockholm Sweden
label.profile: Swedish publishing company established in 1936, which received a new articles of association in 2018.

label.name: Le Poste Parisien
label.profile: Former headquarter and studios of a private radio-company situated in Paris at 116 bis, Avenue des Champs Elysées, Paris 8. After WW2 french authorities banned non-public radio broadcasting and the facilities were used by the still existing company "Le Poste Parisien" as post-production studios for feature films. Also known as studio for overdubbing french dialogue to foreign product (primarily TV features). The location right next to the famous "Lido" was bought in 1973 by [a1226558] / [l275766].

label.name: Warner Chappell Music Scandinavia
label.profile: Name variations: - Warner Chappell Music Scandinavia - Warner Chappell Music Publishing Scandinavia - Warner Chappell Music Scand. - Warner Chappel Scandinavia - Warner Chappell Scand - Warner/Chappell Music Scandinavia - Warner/Chappell Music Scand - Warner/Chappell Scandinavia - Warner/Chappell Scand - Warner-Chappell Music Scandinavia - Warner-Chappell Scandinavia

label.name: Editions Panache
label.profile: French publisher, most likely duplicate of [l=Panache (2)]

label.name: N.E.E.B.
label.profile: Publisher. Short for [l277857].

label.name: Isabelle music
label.profile: French publishing company. Name variation: - Isabell music

label.name: Arcona Musikverlag
label.profile: Publisher

label.name: Sony/ATV Music Publishing Scandinavia KB
label.contactinfo: Tjärhovsgatan 4 116 21 Stockholm Sweden
label.profile:
Full legal entity name: Sony/ATV Music Publishing (Scandinavia) Kommanditbolag
Name variants: - Sony/ATV Music Publishing Scandinavia KB - Sony/ATV Music Publishing Scand. KB - Sony/atv Music Publishing Scandinavia Kb
See also: [l292840]

label.name: Rodahal, Kerkrade
label.contactinfo: Europaplein 1 6461 AD Kerkrade the Netherlands
label.profile: Multifunctional event centre in Kerkrade, the Netherlands. Opened in 1966 and designed by Dutch architect Laurens Bissheroux. Its 'hanging roof ' design is regarded as iconic and from the side it resembles a bow tie, which earned it its nickname "ut sjtriksje" in the local Limburg dialect. The design also refers to the landscape and hills of Limburg. The Rodahal is widely known throughout the Netherlands due to it being the location of the annual Schlagerfestival and within the world of wind band music as the main location of the famous [l931801] which is held every 4 years in Kerkrade.

label.name: Marine Ballroom, Steel Pier, Atlantic City
Onyx Club
label.profile: Recorded At

label.name: Летов
label.contactinfo: gentr5787@ukr.net
label.profile: Летов is a sublabel of Genetic Trance emphasizing media in a quirky/post-modern format. It can be passworded, corrupted files, very small or large files, non-music, silence, .exe files, conceptual releases or just anything. Летов is influenced by [l=Летпав], a defunct platform with similar aesthetics.

label.name: Francis Salabert Editions
label.profile: Publisher
